ops.title: Shared guide fragments live in cc-skills plugin/guides/md/ (pull model, self-source lock pin); consumers render locally against a lock — hand-editing a rendered artifact without fixing the source is the canonical way to break the Guides check

ops.body:
As of 2026-07-11 the pipeline is PULL-model: the push-model fan-out (fleet.json, guides-fan-out.yml) was retired (cc-skills "chore(guides)!: retire the push model — delete fleet.json"). Current truth:

- Shared fragments live in `cc-skills/plugin/guides/md/*.md` ONLY — the old lockstep `guides/` twin directory no longer exists. cc-skills' OWN AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md are now rendered artifacts on the pipeline (self-migrated), composed from those same fragments.
- Consumers (e.g. cc-context) compose artifacts from `.claude/fragments/<target>/layout.toml` mixing repo-local `*.fragment.md` with imports like `cc-skills:ccx`. `cc-guides render` resolves each source to a commit and records it in `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock` (also pins the renderer `version`); the repo's Guides workflow runs `cc-guides check --diff` against that lock.
- **Local render is now the sanctioned fix** for a STALE check ("run 'cc-guides render' locally and commit the fragments, artifacts, and lock together") — the old "never hand-render" rule died with the push model; the lock's `version` (0.1.13+) guards against a stale binary (match `cc-guides --version` before rendering).
- **cc-skills imports ITSELF at `github:yasyf/cc-skills@main`**, so any fragment push there needs a follow-up render+commit just to bump the self-source lock pin — byte-identical artifacts still check STALE when the lock's pinned sha is behind the fragment content.
- **The canonical failure mode (live 2026-07-11):** a session hand-edits a consumer's rendered AGENTS.md (the v0.11.0 `--regex` wording) without touching the cc-skills source → Guides red on every subsequent push, and a naive re-render REVERTS the edit. The fix is source-first: edit `cc-skills/plugin/guides/md/<frag>.md`, ship cc-skills (plus its lock-bump commit), then `cc-guides render` in the consumer and verify the wording survived before committing.
- jj trap (still applies): in a shared working copy based behind main, `jj commit <fileset>` carves onto the STALE base; fix with `jj rebase -r <change> -d main`, then address by CHANGE id.

Related: [[ccx-grep-engine-guard-rewrites]], [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]], [[ccx-web-op-family]].
